title: What is a FLOP?
concept: A FLOP (Floating Point Operation) measures the amount of raw math calculation needed to train or run a model (e.g. multiplying two floats).

exercises.prompt: What does a computer hosting an AI model actually do to generate a response?

options.text: Perform trillions of simple math calculations
Search the web like a human using a browser
Read physical books using a camera sensor

exercises.explain: AI models process language, images, and logic by converting everything into numbers and doing billions of simple math equations.

cards.title: AI is Just Math!
cards.body: AI models seem to speak and think like humans, but under the hood, they are just doing a massive amount of basic school math. Let's look at the basic building block of AI work: the FLOP!

cards.title: What is a FLOP?
cards.body: A FLOP stands for 'Floating Point Operation'. It is a single, basic math calculation involving decimals, such as multiplying 3.14 by 2.71. It is the fundamental unit used to measure the amount of computational work required to train or run an AI.

cards.title: The Kitchen Analogy
cards.body: Think of a FLOP like a single chop of a chef's knife when preparing a meal. If a recipe (an AI model) requires millions of chops (FLOPs) to prepare, it will take more time and energy to finish the dish!

cards.title: FLOPs in Action
cards.body: When you ask a large AI model to write a poem, your computer doesn't just read words. It performs billions or even trillions of FLOPs—tiny math calculations—behind the scenes in a fraction of a second to predict the next word.

cards.title: FLOP vs. FLOPS
cards.body: People often confuse FLOP (a unit of math work) with FLOPS (Floating Point Operations Per Second), which measures speed. A FLOP is the actual calculation itself, not how fast the computer does it!

cards.title: Scale of FLOPs
cards.body: We can compare how computational work (FLOPs) scales from small tasks to massive AI systems.

right.text: Training a modern chatbot takes sextillions of FLOPs across thousands of chips.

left.text: Adding two numbers on a phone calculator takes just a few FLOPs.

exercises.prompt: What does the acronym FLOP stand for?
exercises.explain: FLOP stands for Floating Point Operation, which is the basic unit of mathematical computation involving numbers with decimal points.

exercises.prompt: True or False: A FLOP is a measure of how fast your internet connection is.
exercises.explain: A FLOP is a unit of computational work (the math calculations). Speed is measured in FLOPS (FLOPs per second).

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to complete the definition of a FLOP:
exercises.sentence: A FLOP measures a single basic decimal _______ calculation done by a computer.
exercises.explain: Every FLOP represents one decimal math calculation. AI models perform trillions of these calculations to understand language and generate responses.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following scenarios requires the largest number of FLOPs?

options.text: Adding two whole numbers (1 + 1) using a simple calculator app
Training a massive AI chatbot on trillions of words from the internet
Storing a small text file containing a shopping list on a USB drive

exercises.explain: Running a tiny model to add two whole numbers requires very little math, whereas training a giant chatbot with billions of parameters requires an enormous amount of decimal calculations (FLOPs).

exercises.prompt: If you are measuring the total number of FLOPs used to train a brand new AI model, what are you actually counting?

options.text: The total number of individual math steps, like multiplying decimal numbers, performed by the computer.
The speed of the internet connection used to download the AI weights.
The total amount of storage space the AI model file takes up on a hard drive.
The number of human testers needed to evaluate the AI's answers.

exercises.explain: To calculate how many FLOPs a model needs, we count all the individual steps of floating-point math (like multiplying decimal values) that happen during training or running.
